revision.text:
[[ja:姉崎二子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun | image = File:Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun, funkyu.JPG | part_of = {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Anesaki 1762 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.480556|N|140.052222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-12 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | Length = 103m (estimated reconstructed length: 110m) | built = Early to mid-5th century | burial = Rear mound: 1 wooden coffin, direct burial
Front mound: 1 wooden coffin, direct burial | burial goods = Many grave goods, including {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | designation1 = {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Historic Site|ja|千葉県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("Futagozuka Kofun") | Historic Site = Stone pillow (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=28|coordinates=49.73|lon_deg=140|lon_min=3|lon_sec=8.47|mark=Keyhole mound.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|49.73|N|140|03|8.47|E|qq=}}|qq=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun''' (姉崎二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in the {{ill|Anesaki district|ja|姉崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki district|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara, Chiba|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Historic Site|ja|千葉県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The stone pillow from the kofun is a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Overview == The kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus located on a sandy ridge near the lower reaches of the {{ill|Yōrō River|en|Yōrō River|qid=Q8062667|lt=Yōrō River|1=Yōrō River}}, at an elevation of approximately 5 meters. Part of the {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}}, it was constructed during the early to mid-5th century ({{ill|Mi11le Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mi11le Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). The mound is partially preserved, with the eastern side relatively intact and the western side altered due to residential development. Multiple excavations have been conducted, revealing the site's historical significance. The tumulus measures 103 meters in its current state, with an estimated reconstructed length of 110 meters. It has a three-tiered structure, and traces of cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} rows have been found. The burial chambers in the rear and front mounds contained wooden coffins directly buried, but no remains of coffins or burial facilities were discovered during the 1947 excavation. Numerous grave goods, including a stone pillow Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, were recovered and are currently housed in the {{ill|Kokugakuin University Museum|ja|國學院大學博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University Museum|1=Kokugakuin University Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q81916990}}. == Excavation History == * 1916: Mentioned in the ''Ichihara District Gazetteer,'' the first record of the kofun. * 1945: burial goods unearthed during tree root extraction. * 1947: First archaeological excavation by Kokugakuin University archaeologists, led by {{ill|Iwao Ōwa|ja|大場磐雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao Ōwa|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated a {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Historic Site|ja|千葉県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1974: The stone pillow was Historic Site1 a National Important designation1. * 2005-2014: Several confirmation surveys conducted during residential developments. == Kofun Details == The kofun's area are as follows: * Length: 110 meters (reconstructed); 103 meters (current). * Rear mound diameter: 55 meters. Erosion and later modifications have altered the type, particularly around the edges. While early estimates placed its length at 114 meters, subsequent surveys revised this to 110 meters. File:Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun, koenbu.JPG|Top of the rear mound File:Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun, funkyu.JPG|View from the rear toward the front == burial goods == File:姉崎二子塚古墳出土 石枕.JPG|Stone pillow (National Important designation1), displayed at the Kokugakuin University Museum File:姉崎二子塚古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods displayed at the Kokugakuin University Museum File:姉崎二子塚古墳出土 虺竜文鏡.JPG|''Kyoryūmon'' mirror (at Asuka Historical Museum exhibition) burial goods discovered during the 1947 excavation include: ; Rear mound * 3 copper mirrors (1 intact, 2 fragments) * 8 beads (7 jadeite, 1 steatite) * Amber beads, glass beads, and iron weapons * Stone items, including finely carved tools ; Front mound * 2 swords, 2 silver belt decorations, and an ornate stone pillow. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 === * Stone pillow (with attached decorative flowers) - Designated in 1974, stored at the Kokugakuin University Museum. === Chiba Prefectural Historic Site === * Futagozuka Kofun - Designated in 1968. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Ichihara Archaeological Research Center|ja|市原市埋蔵文化財調査センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihara Archaeological Research Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ichihara, Chiba) - Displays a replica of the stone pillow and other finds. * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Museum|ja|國學院大學博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University Museum|1=Kokugakuin University Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q81916990}} (Tokyo) - Houses and displays the burial goods. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Anesaki Kofun Group|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Kofun Group|qq=}} * {{ill|Kami-Agari-no-Kokuzo|ja|上海上国造|1=Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Agari-no-Kokuzo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-042.html Futagozuka Kofun - Chiba Prefecture Website] * [http://www.city.ichihara.chiba.jp/maibun/isekifile30.htm Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun - Ichihara Archaeological Research Center] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} [[ja:姉崎二子塚古墳]] {{translated page|ja|姉崎二子塚古墳|version=99995038|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Anesaki Futago-zuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11447258}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:市原市の歴史]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]]
